Catalog Description

Special Topics in... Credit 0 to 4. Selected topics in an identified area of computer

science. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: Approval of instructor.

Additional Course Details

NA

Course Prerequisites

Prerequisite/Corequisite(s):  Approval of instructor.

Course Learning Outcomes

Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

Describe and implement various algorithms for logical inference,

including unification, forward-chaining, backward-chaining, and

resolution.

Write programs in PROLOG to solve various types of problems, and

understand how negation-as-failure and the cut operator work.

Characterize the computational complexity of logical inference, and

describe algorithmic heuristics for improving the efficiency of inference.

Explain different methods for Default and Non-monotonic Reasoning,

and its utility in building real-world knowledge-based systems.

Describe and implement various algorithms for symbolic planning,

including forward state-space search, goal regression, and partial order

planning.

Characterize the computational complexity of symbolic planning, and

describe algorithmic heuristics for improving the efficiency of

generating plans.

Describe and implement methods for making probabilistic inferences with

Bayesian Networks, and for solving Markov Decision Problems.

Grading Policy

Programming assignments: 80%.

Final project: 20%

The cutoff for an `A' will be >= 90% of total score, >= 80% for a `B', >= 70% for

a `C', >= 60% for a `D', and below 60% for an 'F'.

Late Work Policy

Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not

considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy ( Student Rule 7 ).

assignment

chaining

Theorem, QBFs, Semi-decidability of FOL)

types of applications/programming paradigms

with PROLOG

AI Statement

This course assumes that all work submitted by students will be generated by the students

themselves. Students should not have another person/entity do the writing of any substantive

portion of an assignment for them, which includes hiring a person or a company to write

assignments and using artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT or GitHub Copilot.

Course Specific Makeup Work Policy
